channel.title: x86_64 on Jin's blog
channel.description: Recent content in x86_64 on Jin's blog

item.title: Handle Interrupt on x86-64 kernel with zig
item.description: Preface On x86_64, if we want to handle interrupts, we need to define IDT(Interrupt Descriptor Table), it has 256 elements. And we also need to set IDTR(Interrupt Descriptor Table Register). In this article, we won’t discuss setting up IDT, that would be too long. We just discuss how to handle interruptions gracefully, pay attention, what we are talking about here is the interrupt handling function, in other words, what you want to fill in the ide (it only fills part of the idt).
